description_long: This is a delicate flower bud with a teardrop shape, featuring creamy white petals tinged with soft pink hues, especially concentrated along the edges and tips. The petals are tightly closed, forming a smooth, rounded surface with subtle veining visible in the lighter areas. At the base, several green sepals extend outward, providing a contrasting foundation to the pastel petals above. The flower appears fresh and youthful, with no signs of blooming yet, and the sepals are slightly pointed and lanceolate in shape. The overall structure is compact, with the petals overlapping in a layered, whorled arrangement. This type of flower bud is commonly seen in urban parks, gardens, or as part of decorative landscaping.
description: A closed, teardrop-shaped flower bud with creamy white petals tinged with pink and green lanceolate sepals at the base. The petals are tightly layered, giving the bud a smooth, compact appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rounded tip of the bud with pink edges and green sepals radiating outward at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the smooth, pale surface of the bud and the pointed sepals extending downward.
description_view_2: Another angled view reveals the gentle curve of the petals and the subtle pink gradient along one side.
description_view_3: Rear view displays the seam where two main petals meet, with pronounced pink coloring and visible sepals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled configuration, tightly overlapping and enclosing the bud in a spiral-like manner, with no clear radial symmetry due to the closed state.
